sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a stone on a hearth, on which meat was roasted or bread baked. 1Ki 19:6, עֻגַּת רְצָפִים “a cake cooked upon the stones.” The Rabbins understand hot coal, compare רֶשֶׁף.

(2) [Rezeph], pr. name of a city subdued by the Assyrians, Isa 37:12 perhaps Ῥησάφα of Ptolemy (page 350, ed. Basil), situated in Palmyrene; Arabic رُصَافَةُ.
